mj_eu.title: Morning Juice – Europe Session Prep
mj_eu.summary:
• President Trump is preparing for an extended US Naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to intensify economic pressure on Iran.
• Australia's inflation stayed above the RBA's 2-3% target, driven by higher fuel costs, suggesting continued monetary tightening.
• The UAE's decision to quit OPEC challenges the cartel's influence in a rapidly changing global oil market.
• The UK faces potential recession and aggressive interest rate hikes if the Middle East crisis escalates, according to NIESR.
• Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, including the Strait of Hormuz blockade and OPEC dynamics, are impacting global oil prices.

wrap.title: Tech Selloff Hits Stocks as AI Doubts Resurface – US Market Wrap
wrap.summary:
• US stocks fell, with the S&P 500 pulling back from a record and the Nasdaq 100 dropping 1%, while yields rose and oil climbed above $111.
• A tech selloff was driven by concerns over AI investment returns after reports of OpenAI missing user and sales targets.
• Brent crude surged above $111 per barrel amid fears that a prolonged peace process could keep the Strait of Hormuz closed.
• Expectations of the Fed holding rates for a third consecutive meeting, due to inflation and a resilient labor market, led to falling short-dated Treasuries.
• JPMorgan CEO Dimon cautioned that a potential credit downturn could be more severe than anticipated, despite strong bank earnings.
• Major tech earnings from Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, and Apple are anticipated, following the tech sector's weakness.

mj_us.title: Morning Juice – US Session Prep (29th April)
mj_us.summary:
• Treasuries fluctuated, Brent crude climbed above $115 per barrel (a four-week high), and the dollar edged higher ahead of the Fed's rate decision.
• The FOMC Rate Decision and Fed Chair Powell's press conference are the day's main events, with rates forecast to hold at 3.75%.
• US Durable Goods for March Prelim is expected at 0.5%, following a prior -1.3%.
• The Bank of Canada is also expected to hold its rate at 2.25%, followed by Governor Macklem's press conference.
• Major tech earnings are due from Amazon ($177.21B revenue est.), Google ($91.56B revenue est.), META ($55.51B revenue est.), and Microsoft ($81.46B revenue est.).
• Qualcomm ($10.56B revenue est.), Ebay ($3.03B revenue est.), and Ford ($39.58B revenue est.) also report earnings.
